```json
    "conclusion": "重构 `ty::Alias` 模式匹配，适配新的内部结构。",
    "description": "代码变更的主要目的是适配 `ty::Alias` 的内部结构重构。新的结构将 `AliasKind` 嵌入到 `AliasTy` 内部，因此模式匹配需要从 `ty::Alias(kind, alias_ty)` 改为 `ty::Alias(alias_ty)`，并通过 `alias_ty.kind` 访问具体的 `AliasKind`（如 `ty::Opaque { def_id }`）。此次变更涉及多个文件，更新了所有相关的模式匹配和字段访问，以确保代码在新结构下正常工作。",
    "evidence": "例如，在文件 `compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/diagnostics/opaque_types.rs` 中，模式从 `if let ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, opaque) = *ty.kind()` 改为 `if let ty::Alias(opaque @ ty::AliasTy { kind: ty::Opaque { def_id }, .. }) = *ty.kind()`，后续对 `opaque.def_id` 的访问也相应更新为 `def_id`。",
    "advice": "变更看起来是正确且一致的。建议在合并前运行完整的测试套件，以确保所有用例（特别是涉及不透明类型、关联类型和类型别名的用例）都能通过。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]
```

    "description": "代码变更的主要目的是将旧的`ty::Alias(kind, alias_ty)`匹配模式更新为新的`ty::Alias(alias_ty)`模式，其中`alias_ty`是一个`AliasTy`结构体，其`kind`字段包含了原来的`kind`信息（如`Opaque`、`Projection`等）。这是一个大规模的重构，涉及编译器多个模块。从提交信息看，这是`ty::Alias`重构的一部分，旨在修复clippy警告并添加`#[track_caller]`。变更本身是结构性的，旨在保持功能不变。",
    "evidence": "例如，在文件`compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/diagnostics/opaque_types.rs`中，模式从`if let ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, opaque) = *ty.kind()` 变为 `if let ty::Alias(opaque @ ty::AliasTy { kind: ty::Opaque { def_id }, .. }) = *ty.kind()`。类似的变化遍布所有变更文件。",
    "advice": "审查应重点关注重构是否引入了功能回归，例如：1. 访问`def_id`的路径是否正确（现在通过`alias_ty.kind.def_id()`或解构获得）。2. 对`args`的访问是否一致。3. 所有分支是否都得到了正确处理。从代码变更看，模式匹配的更新看起来是系统性的和正确的。建议运行完整的测试套件以确保没有引入回归。",

review:
```json
[
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "conclusion": "统一单变体枚举与多变体枚举的匹配行为，确保始终读取判别式",
    "description": "代码变更的核心目的是修改Rust编译器对单变体枚举（single-variant enums）的匹配语义。变更前，对于单变体枚举（尤其是非穷尽枚举），在某些情况下（如闭包捕获分析、借用检查）可能不会读取其判别式（discriminant），这可能导致未定义行为（UB）无法被检测到，或者产生不一致的借用捕获行为。变更后，无论枚举是否只有一个变体，只要它是枚举类型（`def.is_enum()`），在模式匹配时都会强制读取其判别式。这确保了操作语义的一致性，并使得Miri等工具能够正确检测无效的枚举标签或未初始化的内存。",
```

commits:
[{'sha': '92775988b4215c77a26018acb23d4fbdeff52e2b', 'message': "Change the column selection strategy in `pick_test()` to reduce redundant tests

Previously, `pick_test()` always selected the first match pair of the first
candidate. This could lead to suboptimal match trees when many arms share
a value for a field that is not listed first in the pattern. For
example, in `match p { P { x: 1, y: 0 }, P { x: 2, y: 0 }, .. }`, the
compiler would switch on `x` first and then redundantly check `y == 0`
in every branch.

Now, `pick_test()` iterates over the first candidate's match pairs and picks
the one whose testable value is shared across the largest number of
consecutive candidates. In the example above, `y: 0` appears in all
arms (consecutive count = 3), so we test `y` first, check it once, and
then switch on `x` - producing a smaller match tree."}, {'sha': '064b0707356b96c1fe5b570b24b7356ed6f8a27d', 'message': "Add a MIR test for struct fields' order independence in match lowering"}]

[{'sha': 'ece42cb13d9d26faba4221f8e2eb9f550ebad528', 'message': "Remove `rfail` support from incremental tests

Incremental revisions beginning with `rfail` would cause the incremental test
runner to build the test program, expecting success, and then run the test
program, expecting failure.

Expecting incremental tests to fail at runtime is of questionable utility,
because in almost all cases an equivalent test program can be made to succeed
at runtime instead.

Removing `rfail` support is a small step towards cleaning up compiletest's
incremental test runner, and its overall handling of pass/fail expectations.

There was one impenetrable regression test using `rfail` revisions, which has
been converted into a run-make test."}]

    "description": "代码变更的主要目的是为查询系统引入一个名为 `handle_cycle_error` 的新查询修饰符。该修饰符允许特定查询在发生循环时使用自定义的错误处理函数，而不是默认的终止编译行为。同时，变更重构了查询描述函数的生成方式，将其从独立的辅助模块移入查询修饰符结构体，简化了代码结构。",
    "evidence": "在 `compiler/rustc_macros/src/query.rs` 中，`QueryModifiers` 结构体新增了 `handle_cycle_error: Option<Ident>` 字段，`parse_query_modifiers` 函数新增了对 `handle_cycle_error` 标识符的解析，`make_modifiers_stream` 函数新增了 `handle_cycle_error` 变量的处理。在 `compiler/rustc_middle/src/query/modifiers.rs` 中新增了 `handle_cycle_error` 结构体的文档。在 `compiler/rustc_middle/src/queries.rs` 中，多个查询（如 `check_representability`, `variances_of` 等）新增了 `handle_cycle_error` 修饰符。在 `compiler/rustc_query_impl/src/handle_cycle_error.rs` 中，原有的 `specialize_query_vtables` 函数被移除，改为为每个使用该修饰符的查询定义独立的处理函数（如 `fn_sig`, `check_representability` 等）。在 `compiler/rustc_query_impl/src/query_impl.rs` 的宏定义中，根据 `handle_cycle_error` 字面量值，动态选择调用自定义处理函数或默认处理函数。",

commits:
[{'sha': 'ff1795fe4938d3de8588e9547a6749efed24d34c', 'message': 'Remove `Clone` impl for `StableHashingContext`.

`HashStable::hash_stable` takes a `&mut Hcx`. In contrast,
`ToStableHashKey::to_stable_hash_key` takes a `&Hcx`. But there are
some places where the latter calls the former, and due to the mismatch a
`clone` call is required to get a mutable `StableHashingContext`.

This commit changes `to_stable_hash_key` to instead take a `&mut Hcx`.
This eliminates the mismatch, the need for the clones, and the need for
the `Clone` impls.'}]

commits:
[{'sha': 'ff1795fe4938d3de8588e9547a6749efed24d34c', 'message': 'Remove `Clone` impl for `StableHashingContext`.

`HashStable::hash_stable` takes a `&mut Hcx`. In contrast,
`ToStableHashKey::to_stable_hash_key` takes a `&Hcx`. But there are
some places where the latter calls the former, and due to the mismatch a
`clone` call is required to get a mutable `StableHashingContext`.

This commit changes `to_stable_hash_key` to instead take a `&mut Hcx`.
This eliminates the mismatch, the need for the clones, and the need for
the `Clone` impls.'}]

+    /// [Definition 144] in Chapter 3 of the Unicode Standard.
+    ///
+    /// [Definition 144]: https://www.unicode.org/versions/latest/core-spec/chapter-3/#G53513
+    ///
+    /// Same as `a.to_casefold() == b.to_casefold()`,
+    /// but without allocating. See that method's documentation,
+    /// and [`char::to_casefold()`],
+    /// for more information about case folding.
+    ///
+    /// No normalization (e.g. NFC) is performed,
+    /// so visually and semantically identical strings
+    /// might still compare unequal. In addition,
+    /// this method is independent of language/locale,
+    /// so the special behavior of  I/ı/İ/i
+    /// in Turkish and Azeri is not handled.
+    ///
+    /// # Examples
+    ///
+    /// ```
+    /// #![feature(casefold)]
+    /// assert!("Ferris".eq_ignore_case("FERRIS"));

commits:
[{'sha': '516ac2a5ad5289b8a77dc915edd6078b6d64b611', 'message': 'Use interior mutability for `StableHashingContext::caching_source_map`.

`StableHashingContext::caching_source_map` is currently externally
mutated by `span_hash_stable`, which is reachable from many
`hash_stable` methods. But the mutation that happens is just some
internal caching, via `CachingSourceMap`. Conceptually it feels like a
read-only lookup of source map data.

This commit changes the field to interior mutability. This will let
`hash_stable` use `&Hcx` instead of `&mut Hcx`. The switch involves
replacing the existing `StableHashingContext::source_map` method with a
new `StableHashingContxt::span_data_to_lines_and_cols` method, because
returning a `&mut` was problematic with the use of `RefCell`. The
existing `CachingSourceMapView::span_data_to_lines_and_cols` now returns
an `Arc`, but that seems fine because the nearby
`CachingSourceMapView::byte_pos_to_line_and_col` already does that.'}, {'sha': 'bd9b5ae92c7e2b78e3502277ff9aaff575a60c88', 'message': 'Pass `&hcx` instead of `&mut Hcx` to `hash_stable`.

This was enabled by the previous commit. A few related functions also
get a similar change.'}, {'sha': '79b99e682951737086a811a8a725c520c2ccceaa', 'message': "Avoid cloning of `StableHashingContext`.

It's no longer needed now that `hash_stable` takes a `&hcx` instead of
`&mut hcx`."}]

commits:
[{'sha': '516ac2a5ad5289b8a77dc915edd6078b6d64b611', 'message': 'Use interior mutability for `StableHashingContext::caching_source_map`.

`StableHashingContext::caching_source_map` is currently externally
mutated by `span_hash_stable`, which is reachable from many
`hash_stable` methods. But the mutation that happens is just some
internal caching, via `CachingSourceMap`. Conceptually it feels like a
read-only lookup of source map data.

This commit changes the field to interior mutability. This will let
`hash_stable` use `&Hcx` instead of `&mut Hcx`. The switch involves
replacing the existing `StableHashingContext::source_map` method with a
new `StableHashingContxt::span_data_to_lines_and_cols` method, because
returning a `&mut` was problematic with the use of `RefCell`. The
existing `CachingSourceMapView::span_data_to_lines_and_cols` now returns
an `Arc`, but that seems fine because the nearby
`CachingSourceMapView::byte_pos_to_line_and_col` already does that.'}, {'sha': 'bd9b5ae92c7e2b78e3502277ff9aaff575a60c88', 'message': 'Pass `&hcx` instead of `&mut Hcx` to `hash_stable`.

This was enabled by the previous commit. A few related functions also
get a similar change.'}, {'sha': '79b99e682951737086a811a8a725c520c2ccceaa', 'message': "Avoid cloning of `StableHashingContext`.

It's no longer needed now that `hash_stable` takes a `&hcx` instead of
`&mut hcx`."}]

commits:

LLVM has moved memcmp expansion in the pipeline, resulting in the bcmp
call being expanded into loads and register comparisons, which breaks
the test.

these arrays were being passed as pointer arguments, which can be done
more directly.'}]

```json
    "conclusion": "为 `ill_formed_attribute_input` 添加了更详细的帮助信息，特别是针对类似 `#![allow]` 的用法。",
    "description": "在 `test_attrs.rs` 中，当 `ArgParser::List` 包含单个无参数的元项时，会生成一个帮助信息。然而，生成的帮助信息（`item.path().to_string()`）可能不是用户期望的 lint 名称。例如，对于 `#[ignore(clippy::single_match)]`，`item.path()` 可能返回 `\"clippy::single_match\"`，这确实是正确的 lint 名称。但是，如果路径更复杂（例如包含嵌套模块），或者用户期望的是完整的属性路径（如 `clippy::single_match`），`to_string()` 的表示可能不够清晰或准确。此外，如果 `list.single()` 返回 `None`（即列表为空或多个项），或者 `item.meta_item()` 返回 `None`（即项是字面量），则 `help` 为 `None`，这可能导致帮助信息在某些情况下缺失。",
    "evidence": "let help = list.single().and_then(|item| item.meta_item()).and_then(|item| {\n    item.args().no_args().ok()?;\n    Some(item.path().to_string())\n});",
    "advice": "建议验证 `item.path().to_string()` 是否确实生成了用户友好的 lint 名称。可以考虑使用 `item.path().segments.last().unwrap().ident.to_string()` 来获取最后的标识符，或者确保路径的字符串表示是完整的。同时，可以添加日志或断言来验证在测试用例 `#[ignore(clippy::single_match)]` 中，`help` 的值是 `\"clippy::single_match\"`。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Medium"

  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "conclusion": "重构了 `AcceptContext` 中的错误处理方法，并添加了 `warn_ill_formed_attribute_input_with_help` 方法。",
    "description": "在 `context.rs` 中，新增了大量错误处理辅助方法（如 `expected_string_literal`、`expected_integer_literal` 等），这些方法通过调用 `emit_parse_error` 来统一处理错误。这提高了代码的可维护性和一致性。然而，`warn_ill_formed_attribute_input_with_help` 方法中，`help` 参数的类型是 `Option<String>`，但在 `IllFormedAttributeInputHelp` 结构体中，`lint` 字段是 `String`。这意味着如果 `help` 是 `Some(String)`，它将被直接用作 `lint` 字段的值。在 `test_attrs.rs` 中，`help` 是从 `item.path().to_string()` 生成的，这可能导致帮助信息中的 lint 名称与用户输入的属性路径不完全一致。",
    "evidence": "pub(crate) fn warn_ill_formed_attribute_input_with_help(\n    &mut self,\n    lint: &'static Lint,\n    help: Option<String>,\n) {\n    let suggestions = self.suggestions();\n    let span = self.attr_span;\n    self.emit_lint(\n        lint,\n        AttributeLintKind::IllFormedAttributeInput { suggestions, docs: None, help },\n        span,\n    );\n}",
    "advice": "建议在 `test_attrs.rs` 中，确保 `help` 字符串的格式符合 `IllFormedAttributeInputHelp` 的期望。例如，如果用户输入是 `#[ignore(clippy::single_match)]`，帮助信息应该明确提示使用 `#![allow(clippy::single_match)]`。可以检查 `item.path().to_string()` 是否返回 `\"clippy::single_match\"`。如果不是，可能需要调整路径的表示方式。",

  },

    "conclusion": "在多个文件中更新了 `IllFormedAttributeInput` 结构，添加了 `help` 字段。",
    "description": "为了支持更详细的帮助信息，在 `AttributeLintKind::IllFormedAttributeInput` 枚举变体中添加了 `help: Option<String>` 字段。这需要在多个文件中进行更新：`doc.rs`、`validate_attr.rs`、`diagnostics.rs`、`lints.rs` 和 `lib.rs`。这种设计是合理的，因为它允许在需要时提供额外的上下文信息。然而，在 `doc.rs` 和 `validate_attr.rs` 中，`help` 被硬编码为 `None`，这可能意味着在这些上下文中还没有实现帮助信息。这可能导致不一致性，即某些 `ill_formed_attribute_input` 警告有帮助信息，而另一些没有。",
```

[{'sha': '45a0211832683496265140079e4c5ce99f8ea286', 'message': "Skip `StorageDead` items on unwind for coroutines without any yields

For coroutines, `StorageDead` operations were unconditionally added to
the unwind path. For the included test, each array element's unwind chain
includes unique `StorageDead` locals, preventing tail-sharing between chains.
This produces O(n^2) MIR basic blocks for large aggregate initialisers
(e.g. sum(1 to n) = ~50M blocks for n = 10,000).

`StorageDead` on the unwind path is needed for correctness: without it,
the coroutine drop handler may re-drop locals already dropped during
unwinding. However, this can only happen when the coroutine body contains
yield points, because locals can only span a yield if one exists.

Fix: scan the THIR at construction time for `Yield` expressions.
When none are found (for example, in an async function with no `.await`),
set `storage_dead_on_unwind = false` on all drops, skipping `StorageDead`
on the unwind path, which makes the MIR block count linear rather than quadratic."}, {'sha': '77b6066447e3a3ff46d1702628a76b1617ae493d', 'message': 'Add a regression test for MIR blow-up in an `async` function with a large `Vec<String>` construction

Add a run-make test that generates and compiles an `async` function
returning a `vec!["string".to_string(), …]` with 10,000 elements.
Without the preceding fix, this example would produce tens of millions
of MIR basic blocks and take minutes to compile.
With the fix in place, it compiles in a few seconds.

The timeout specified in the test makes it so it would fail before the fix applied.'}, {'sha': 'cdbd718f23b3c73370d4e79cdb02729032cbfb64', 'message': "Update the result for tests/mir-opt/async_drop_live_dead.rs as it's an expected change"}]

[{'sha': '45a0211832683496265140079e4c5ce99f8ea286', 'message': "Skip `StorageDead` items on unwind for coroutines without any yields

For coroutines, `StorageDead` operations were unconditionally added to
the unwind path. For the included test, each array element's unwind chain
includes unique `StorageDead` locals, preventing tail-sharing between chains.
This produces O(n^2) MIR basic blocks for large aggregate initialisers
(e.g. sum(1 to n) = ~50M blocks for n = 10,000).

`StorageDead` on the unwind path is needed for correctness: without it,
the coroutine drop handler may re-drop locals already dropped during
unwinding. However, this can only happen when the coroutine body contains
yield points, because locals can only span a yield if one exists.

Fix: scan the THIR at construction time for `Yield` expressions.
When none are found (for example, in an async function with no `.await`),
set `storage_dead_on_unwind = false` on all drops, skipping `StorageDead`
on the unwind path, which makes the MIR block count linear rather than quadratic."}, {'sha': '77b6066447e3a3ff46d1702628a76b1617ae493d', 'message': 'Add a regression test for MIR blow-up in an `async` function with a large `Vec<String>` construction

Add a run-make test that generates and compiles an `async` function
returning a `vec!["string".to_string(), …]` with 10,000 elements.
Without the preceding fix, this example would produce tens of millions
of MIR basic blocks and take minutes to compile.
With the fix in place, it compiles in a few seconds.

The timeout specified in the test makes it so it would fail before the fix applied.'}, {'sha': 'cdbd718f23b3c73370d4e79cdb02729032cbfb64', 'message': "Update the result for tests/mir-opt/async_drop_live_dead.rs as it's an expected change"}]

    "type": "Design",
    "conclusion": "将 Try trait 拆分为 Branch 和 FromOutput，重构了相关代码。",
    "description": "此次变更的核心是将原有的 `Try` trait 拆分为 `Branch` 和 `FromOutput` 两个独立的 trait，并将 `Try` 定义为 `Branch + FromResidual + FromOutput` 的组合。这属于重大的设计重构，旨在分离关注点，可能为未来的扩展（如支持 `&R` 等类型）提供基础。变更范围广泛，涉及编译器、标准库核心模块、测试等多个文件。",
    "evidence": "文件 `library/core/src/ops/try_trait.rs` 中新增了 `pub const trait Branch` 和 `pub const trait FromOutput`，并将 `pub const trait Try` 修改为 `pub const trait Try = [const] Branch + [const] FromResidual + [const] FromOutput;`。",
    "advice": "这是一个设计上的重大变更，需要确保所有相关的编译器内部逻辑、标准库实现以及下游代码都已正确适配。建议进行全面的测试，包括编译测试、单元测试和集成测试，以验证重构的正确性。同时，需要更新相关的文档和错误信息，确保与新的 trait 结构保持一致。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "conclusion": "代码变更中使用了不明确的类型占位符 `<_>`，可能影响可读性。",
    "description": "在多个文件中，代码将 `Try::from_output(...)` 替换为 `<_>::from_output(...)`。这种使用类型占位符 `<_>` 的写法虽然在某些上下文中可以由编译器推断，但可能不如显式指定 trait 或使用完全限定路径清晰，尤其是在复杂的泛型上下文中，可能增加理解难度。",
    "evidence": "例如，在 `library/core/src/option.rs` 中：`<_>::from_output(unsafe { self.as_mut().unwrap_unchecked() })`。在 `library/core/src/iter/traits/iterator.rs` 中：`<_>::from_output(Some(x))` 和 `<_>::from_output(None)`。",
    "advice": "考虑使用更明确的写法，例如 `FromOutput::from_output(...)` 或 `T::from_output(...)`（如果类型 `T` 在上下文中明确）。这可以提高代码的清晰度和可维护性。如果当前写法是 Rust 中处理此情况的惯用方式，且能确保类型推断无误，则可以保留，但建议在代码审查中确认其必要性。",

', "Have a few more PRs that I need to investigate, don't have bandwidth to review this any time soon. @rustbot reroll", 'Note that this still needs approval from the lang team as an experiment before going forward.', "> Thanks for opening the door on resolving things in attributes. I actually want this for other things so it's nice to see happening. > > There are a lot of names like `AttrConstResolution`, `attr_const_res_map`, `attr_const_resolution` etc being used. I'm worried that if someone wants to extend this to resolve other things (not constants) they'll have to change all those names. Can you choose a more general naming scheme? (let's wait for consensus before making big changes though) > > For example maybe just drop the `const` part everywhere and change `AttrConstResolution` to > > ```rust > enum AttrResolution { > Const(...) > } > ``` > > so others can add variants as needed. > > I can't comment on the name resolution part - I'm not familiar with it. > > _[View changes since this review](https://triagebot.infra.rust-lang.org/gh-changes-since/rust-lang/rust/154708/8931f23941cf9ec0093f86a81337873b288344c9..be14df8f1a036d33fcfa1afc9ea9d829a2104154)_ Thanks! Yea as I get further and further into critical-safe aware library writing (offline I'm doing slab-allocator basaed green-thread fiber stacks, with a script that crawls the ELF for exact slab sizing for stack-based futures and async, no more pin box dyn trait), I'm finding more and more machinery that I think really should be rust-native. Being able to build per-target alignment is a BIG one, but I have a lot of others cooking. I've taken the suggestions and code reviews I've gotten, and am about to push.
